response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and distributed systems experience from high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby. However, the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ or Rust for a systems-level routing data pipeline, and Bogdan's C++ experience is limited to a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a university bachelor project — with no Rust at all. His primary stack for the last decade is TypeScript/Node.js, and his GIS work targets NGO data collection rather than production routing tile generation or navigation guidance systems. The technical mismatch on the primary language requirement and the domain-specific pipeline experience is too significant to overlook.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective platform and Green Map web GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-throughput microservices
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and cross-stack versatility
concerns: No production C++ or Rust experience; only dated, limited C++ work from 2014–2015 and a university thesis project
Primary language stack for the last 10+ years is TypeScript/Node.js — not systems-level languages required by this role
GIS experience is scoped to data collection and mapping for NGOs, not routing tile generation, navigation pipelines, or real-time traffic integration
